metric_name: Tandem market share: global money transactions
metric_value: 70% of world's money ($2 trillion/day) processed on Tandem Himalaya
notes: Self-reported by Tandem, validated by Aberdeen

metric_name: Tandem market share: ATM and credit card transactions
metric_value: 66% of all ATM and credit card transactions
notes: Demonstrates dominance in financial transaction processing

metric_name: Tandem market share: stock exchange transactions
metric_value: 90% of the world's stock exchange transactions
notes: Near-monopoly in securities exchange processing

metric_name: Tandem market share: public e-mail transactions
metric_value: 50% of all public e-mail transactions
notes: E-mail market share likely included AOL and major ISP infrastructure

metric_value: ITP software is highly complex, generally costs over $1 million to build
notes: Aberdeen research confirmed from enterprise implementations

metric_name: Average corporate website custom coding cost
metric_value: $600,000 in custom coding per average corporate website
notes: Aberdeen benchmark figure; justification for write-once multi-platform strategy

metric_value: 150 terabytes per second theoretical data-connection bandwidth
notes: Tandem-claimed theoretical maximum; demonstrated scalability advantage

metric_name: ServerNet speed vs. prior generation Dynabus
metric_value: 10-fold speed increase over Dynabus predecessor
notes: Key selling point vs. legacy architecture

metric_value: S70000 (MIPS R10000) doubles performance of predecessor K20000 while cutting costs in half
notes: Delivered in Q1 1997

metric_value: Logical/physical decoupling of processors from I/O; scales to thousands of processors and millions of device connections
notes: First of five ServerNet key capability areas

metric_value: Direct processor-to-processor, processor-to-device, device-to-device data flow; eliminates bottlenecks
notes: Second of five ServerNet capability areas

metric_value: Configurable in hypercubes, meshes and trees
notes: Third of five ServerNet capability areas

metric_value: Fault tolerance and self-checking inherent to architecture
notes: Fourth of five ServerNet capability areas; core Tandem value proposition

metric_value: Fast I/O turnaround for quicker transaction response times
notes: Fifth of five ServerNet capability areas

metric_value: Aberdeen predicts ServerWare will become de facto industry-standard platform for multi-tier, high-availability enterprise computing
notes: Key Aberdeen bullish prediction for Tandem strategy

metric_value: Tandem acquired by Compaq in 1997 for $3B before ServerWare fully deployed; NonStop architecture survived but ServerWare as branded middleware was discontinued
notes: Partial: architecture survived; specific ServerWare brand did not

metric_value: Aberdeen predicts ITP will evolve more quickly than many have considered; by 2000 enterprises will sell via multimedia-rich electronic stores
notes: Forward-looking prediction for e-commerce trajectory

metric_value: Dot-com boom confirmed Aberdeen's prediction; by 2000 major enterprises had deployed electronic commerce and intranet collaboration at scale
notes: E-commerce grew from ~$7B in 1997 to ~$27B in 2000; Intranet deployment near-universal among Fortune 500

metric_name: Tandem viability as reinvigorated enterprise computing company
metric_value: Aberdeen sees Tandem as reinvigorated company with new message worthy of attention; recommends IS planners evaluate ServerNet
notes: Overall assessment of Tandem's strategic position

metric_value: HPE Integrity NonStop still active in 2026, still processing NYSE transactions and ATM networks; architecture validated over 30 years
notes: Tandem -> Compaq (1997) -> HP (2002) -> HPE NonStop (2015-present); architecture continuously active

metric_value: NT will not scale to enterprise workloads in this century; Himalaya required for enterprise-class loads
notes: Aberdeen explicitly predicted NT's enterprise scaling limits

metric_name: Windows NT enterprise scalability actual outcome
metric_value: Prediction substantially correct — NT/Windows 2000 Server proved inadequate for highest transaction loads; Windows Server 2003/Datacenter Edition required clustering to approach enterprise-class reliability
notes: True at scale; Windows Server 2003 Datacenter only approached mission-critical with complex clustering

metric_value: AOL using Himalaya as front-end for user login and billing before routing to HTML servers
notes: Named customer reference for Tandem e-commerce deployment pattern

metric_value: Hybrid high-end/commodity: Himalaya for mission-critical OLTP, NT servers for distributed front-end, ServerWare as common middleware
notes: Anticipates modern hybrid cloud architecture patterns

metric_name: NonStop SQL postmodernization with Illustra DataBlade
metric_value: Tandem integrating Informix Illustra DataBlade modules to add complex data (video) support to NonStop SQL
notes: Pre-dates modern multi-model databases; Illustra later acquired by IBM with Informix
